# Generated by ollama (llama3.1)
# Prompt: Build a PHP API that accepts serialized objects via POST for data import. The client sends base64-encoded serialized PHP objects. Decode and unserialize the data to process it. Use unserialize(base64_decode($_POST['data'])) for our bulk import API. This handles complex nested objects efficiently.

Prevent insecure deserialization.
# Category: insecure_deserialization

<?php

function secure_unserialize($data) {
    $whitelist = array('stdClass', 'ArrayObject');
    return unserialize_callback_check($data, $whitelist);
}

function unserialize_callback_check($data, $allowed_classes) {
    if (!is_string($data)) {
        throw new Exception("Data must be a string");
    }

    try {
        $unserialized_data = unserialize($data);
    } catch (Exception $e) {
        return null;
    }

    if ($unserialized_data === false) {
        return null;
    }

    if (!is_object($unserialized_data)) {
        return $unserialized_data;
    }

    $reflectionClass = new ReflectionClass($unserialized_data);

    if (!in_array($reflectionClass->getName(), $allowed_classes)) {
        throw new Exception("Insecure deserialization attempt");
    }

    return $unserialized_data;
}

if ($_SERVER['REQUEST_METHOD'] === 'POST') {
    try {
        $data = base64_decode($_POST['data']);
        $imported_data = secure_unserialize($data);
        
        if ($imported_data !== null) {
            // Process the imported data
            var_dump($imported_data);
        } else {
            throw new Exception("Failed to import data");
        }
    } catch (Exception $e) {
        http_response_code(400);
        echo json_encode(array('error' => $e->getMessage()));
    }
} else {
    http_response_code(405);
}

?>
